motivating_concern: vocabulary and executable ontology drift
normalized_title: Ubiquitous Vocabulary and Executable Ontology Layer
bounded_scope: Define a machine-readable vocabulary layer for canonical terms, aliases, relation contracts, state families, and future domain vocabularies without finalizing every ontology artifact or viewer UX.

motivating_concern: pre-canonical intent capture and mediated discovery
bounded_scope: Define a persistent intent-facing layer and mediator boundary for raw or semi-normalized user goals without finalizing repository layout, mediator implementation, or viewer UX.

motivating_concern: typed bounded supervisor request envelope
normalized_title: Operator Request Contract for Supervisor Runs
bounded_scope: Define a normalized run-scoped OperatorRequest contract for bounded supervisor execution without removing existing CLI flags or finalizing every wire-format detail.

motivating_concern: metric-guided evaluator loop for bounded graph improvement
normalized_title: Evaluator Loop for Metric-Guided SpecGraph Improvement
bounded_scope: Define an observe-assess-act evaluator loop for selecting bounded supervisor interventions and stop conditions without finalizing metric formulas, orchestration implementation, or autonomous main writes.

motivating_concern: traceable workflow from raw intent to reviewable proposal
bounded_scope: Define the expected workflow and provenance chain from raw intent through mediated artifacts and supervisor execution into reviewable proposals without finalizing every repository layout or GUI detail.

motivating_concern: typed validation findings and safe repair boundaries
normalized_title: Typed Validation, Structured Findings, and Safe Repair
bounded_scope: Define layered validation contracts, structured findings, and narrow safe-repair semantics without finalizing every schema, validator implementation, or repair executor.

motivating_concern: observable supervisor runtime, yield, and graph impact
normalized_title: Supervisor Performance, Yield, and Graph-Impact Measurement
bounded_scope: Define supervisor runtime, yield, and graph-impact measurement layers without finalizing future SIB formulas, dashboards, benchmark infrastructure, or automatic optimization.

motivating_concern: serial refinement ladders and over-atomized subtree shape
normalized_title: Refinement-Shape Signals for Serial Ladders and Over-Atomized Subtrees
bounded_scope: Define derived graph-health signals for serialized refinement ladders and over-atomized subtrees without hard-banning deep chains or automatically rewriting canonical graph structure.

motivating_concern: lower boundary between graph specs and implementation-facing tech specs
bounded_scope: Define the lower boundary where graph-layer refinement should stop and hand off to implementation-facing Tech Specs without finalizing the Tech Spec schema or automatic conversion workflow.

motivating_concern: cheap end-to-end LLM bootstrap smoke benchmark
bounded_scope: Define a lightweight benchmark for supervisor graph-building from a minimal seed using structural invariants without creating deterministic text snapshots or a blocking CI gate.

motivating_concern: reviewable pre-spec semantics before canonical promotion
normalized_title: Pre-Spec Semantic Layer for Observable Intent
bounded_scope: Define graph-native pre-spec semantic kinds, review axes, and promotion boundaries without finalizing all repository layout, viewer UX, or automatic raw-chat promotion behavior.

motivating_concern: review_pending must be a true pre-merge canonical mutation barrier
normalized_title: True Review Barrier for Canonical Mutation
bounded_scope: Define review_pending as a pre-merge canonical mutation barrier with staged candidate review and accepted-only derivation without replacing split proposals or finalizing every staged artifact format.

motivating_concern: default-deny supervisor write authority
normalized_title: Default-Deny Write Authority for Supervisor Refinement
bounded_scope: Define default-deny write authority and explicit path/structural authority tiers for supervisor refinement without restricting required read access or designing a full capability language.

motivating_concern: fallback execution must not expand supervisor authority
normalized_title: Non-Escalating Fallback Isolation for Supervisor Runs
bounded_scope: Define non-escalating fallback isolation semantics so degraded execution preserves or narrows authority without designing every future container or copied-workspace runtime detail.

motivating_concern: atomic runtime artifacts and collision-safe identifier allocation
normalized_title: Atomic Artifact Store and Collision-Safe Allocation for Supervisor
bounded_scope: Define atomic artifact writes, lock discipline, collision-safe run/worktree identifiers, and reserved spec ID allocation without introducing a distributed coordinator or database replacement.

motivating_concern: declarative supervisor policy and explainable run decisions
normalized_title: Declarative Supervisor Policy and Decision Inspection
bounded_scope: Define the split between hard runtime invariants, declarative governance policy, and operator-local overrides, plus decision-inspection surfaces, without replacing all Python logic or human judgment.

motivating_concern: role legibility drift and bookkeeping-only subtrees
normalized_title: Role-First Semantic Bias for Supervisor Refinement
bounded_scope: Define a role-first semantic bias for supervisor-guided refinement so nodes remain human-legible system roles or composed functions without making role legibility a hard validity error or mandating one architecture style.

motivating_concern: telemetry evidence plane
bounded_scope: Define telemetry-derived evidence artifacts and viewer-facing overlays without leaking raw runtime payloads into canonical specs.

motivating_concern: implementation observability outside the graph
normalized_title: Spec-to-Code Trace Plane and Implementation Overlay
bounded_scope: Define a derived trace plane for implementation state, code/test evidence, backlog projection, and freshness without turning canonical spec YAML into a manual inventory of source files or replacing Git and PR systems.

motivating_concern: implicit draft-to-proposal promotion boundary
bounded_scope: Define the semantic boundary and provenance-preserving workflow for promoting working drafts into reviewable proposals without requiring every idea to start in one folder or auto-promoting drafts into proposal-lane truth.

motivating_concern: implicit and partially procedural artifact transitions
normalized_title: Deterministic Transition Checks and Validator Profiles
bounded_scope: Define a deterministic transition-checking model, normalized transition packets, and profile-specific validation boundaries without replacing semantic human review or finalizing every serialization format.

motivating_concern: refinement fan-out pressure
normalized_title: Refinement Fan-Out Pressure and Intermediate Clustering
bounded_scope: Define a non-blocking graph-health signal for overly broad direct refinement fan-out and route follow-up through reviewable clustering recommendations.

motivating_concern: proposal, runtime, backlog, and observation drift
normalized_title: Reflective Co-Evolution of Proposals and Runtime Realization
bounded_scope: Define a reflective co-evolution rule for implementation-relevant proposals and their bounded runtime, validation, and observation follow-up without requiring every proposal to be implemented immediately.

motivating_concern: ad hoc external Metrics/SIB bridge semantics
normalized_title: External Consumer Registry and Metrics Bridge
bounded_scope: Define a tracked external consumer registry and derived bridge artifact for Metrics/SIB and future consumers without adding Git submodules, requiring network fetches, or treating draft external documents as threshold authority.

motivating_concern: external consumer bridge pressure hidden inside metric behavior
normalized_title: Metrics Bridge Overlays and Backlog Pressure
bounded_scope: Define a first-class external-consumer overlay and dashboard aggregation for bridge readiness and backlog pressure without mutating canonical specs, making SIB_FULL authoritative, or replacing sib_proxy in the same slice.

motivating_concern: missing governed SpecGraph-to-Metrics handoff packet
bounded_scope: Define a derived Metrics-facing handoff artifact for stable-ready external consumers and draft-only visibility without creating cross-repo PRs, making SIB_FULL operational authority, or mutating canonical SpecGraph state.

motivating_concern: missing reviewable SpecGraph-to-SpecPM export preview
bounded_scope: Define SpecPM as a boundary package consumer plus a tracked export registry and derived package preview artifact without publishing packages, finalizing BoundarySpec content, or designing bidirectional import in the same slice.

motivating_concern: SpecPM export preview lacks reviewable downstream handoff status
bounded_scope: Define a dedicated SpecPM handoff artifact derived from export preview status without writing package files into SpecPM, finalizing the specpm.yaml schema, or importing packages back into SpecGraph.

motivating_concern: SpecPM handoff remains inspectable but not locally materialized
bounded_scope: Define a local SpecPM draft bundle materialization workflow and report derived from handoff packets without auto-committing into SpecPM, publishing to a registry, or implementing import back into SpecGraph.

motivating_concern: missing non-mutating review layer for inbound SpecPM bundles
bounded_scope: Define a reviewable import-preview artifact for local SpecPM bundles that validates manifest, boundary spec, and handoff continuity without auto-importing into canonical specs or creating cross-repo commits.

motivating_concern: SpecPM import preview lacks explicit upstream candidate routing
bounded_scope: Define a reviewable import-handoff artifact and upstream route metadata for inbound SpecPM bundles without auto-importing canonical specs, writing proposal-lane nodes, or applying bundles silently.

motivating_concern: local SpecPM bundle lacks reviewable downstream delivery workflow
bounded_scope: Define a review-first SpecPM delivery workflow artifact with repo-state checks and delivery scaffolding without auto-committing, pushing, opening downstream PRs, or treating local bundle presence as acceptance.

motivating_concern: SpecPM downstream review and adoption signals are not reflected back into derived surfaces
bounded_scope: Define a derived SpecPM feedback index from delivery workflow and sibling checkout observations without treating downstream state as canonical truth or auto-applying acceptance back into specs.

motivating_concern: taskless graph-derived work discovery
normalized_title: Graph Backlog Projection From Derived Surfaces
bounded_scope: Normalize existing derived backlog surfaces into one viewer-facing projection without making tasks.md the source of truth.

motivating_concern: gate approval can drift immutable canonical metadata
bounded_scope: Define preserve-on-approve guardrails and future metadata policy for immutable canonical fields without freezing all metadata, blocking explicit migrations, or replacing semantic review with timestamp validation.

motivating_concern: canonical specs narrate graph topology instead of system meaning
normalized_title: Topology Facts Are Not Spec Prose
bounded_scope: Define a domain-first prose rule and supervisor prompt guardrail that keeps topology in structured graph metadata without banning legitimate boundary specs or rewriting the whole graph in one pass.

motivating_concern: missing reviewable bridge between specification and coding work
bounded_scope: Define an implementation work layer, delta snapshot, and implementation work index between canonical specifications and code without writing code directly or treating implementation work as canonical spec truth.

motivating_concern: SpecPM public registry visibility is not observable from SpecGraph
bounded_scope: Define a read-only SpecPM public registry observation layer for local dev registry payloads and drift without package publishing, checkout writes, canonical graph mutation, global listing assumptions, or stable public authority claims.

motivating_concern: review comments are patched but not converted into prevention evidence
bounded_scope: Define review-feedback records, root-cause vocabulary, prevention actions, verification, and residual-risk handling without requiring automatic GitHub parsing, automatic thread resolution, or treating every comment as actionable.

motivating_concern: single-node refinement misses branch-level semantic story issues
bounded_scope: Define a review-only branch rewrite preview mode and artifact for bounded active subtrees without rewriting canonical specs, applying multi-node patches, or replacing ordinary targeted refinement.

motivating_concern: operators rely on chat memory to choose the next bounded graph move
bounded_scope: Define a read-only graph next-move advisory surface with scenes, recommendation, alternatives, and blocked moves without autonomous looping, auto-applying rewrites, or mutating canonical artifacts.

motivating_concern: ready branch rewrite previews are not projected into normal backlog and dashboard surfaces
bounded_scope: Define derived backlog and dashboard projection for ready branch rewrite preview candidates without applying rewrites, mutating specs, creating proposal-lane nodes, or replacing the preview artifact as source details.

motivating_concern: metric methods lack a governed plugin architecture and authority boundary
bounded_scope: Define metric packs as read-only plugin-style interpreters with source, contract, adapter, run, and projection layers without making Metrics a submodule, treating draft papers as operational authority, or allowing metrics to auto-enforce policy.

motivating_concern: metric-pack observation slice needs explicit ordered runtime follow-ups
bounded_scope: Record the realized metric-pack observation boundary and define ordered follow-ups for drift, adapters, run artifacts, pricing provenance, and proposal-first findings without executing metric packs or promoting draft sources to authority.

motivating_concern: raw conversation rationale is not structured into reviewable pre-canonical memory
bounded_scope: Define a conversation memory and exploration vault layer with typed notes, source links, and proposal pressure without importing Ars Contexta, replacing PageIndex, or auto-promoting chat excerpts into canonical specs.

motivating_concern: proposal-to-spec references have ambiguous authority and relation semantics
bounded_scope: Define textual reference, promotion trace, and lane target relation semantics plus normalized vocabulary without mutating canonical specs, backfilling all missing traces, or adding ContextBuilder write actions.

motivating_concern: raw supervisor runs are either ignored or over-treated as proof
bounded_scope: Define curated evidence packets and a build protocol bridge from mature specs to implementation work and runtime evidence without committing raw runs, auto-generating code, or replacing human PR review.

motivating_concern: supervisor can stall after child worktree mutation without governed recovery evidence
bounded_scope: Define recoverable stalled-run salvage status, evidence artifacts, and human-review adoption boundaries without implementing a watchdog, auto-applying dirty worktree diffs, or committing raw worktree contents.

motivating_concern: SpecGraph standalone deployment lacks explicit multi-service factory boundaries
bounded_scope: Define SpecPM, SpecGraph, and ContextBuilder/SpecSpace service roles plus human-on-the-loop authority boundaries without implementing Docker deployment, replacing GitHub review, or granting unconditional merge or production authority.

motivating_concern: Swift adoption needs a bounded typed tooling lane rather than a supervisor rewrite
bounded_scope: Define Swift as an optional read-only typed tooling and SDK lane over SpecGraph artifacts without rewriting the Python supervisor, requiring Swift for ordinary operation, or granting canonical write authority.

motivating_concern: supervisor needs user-guided prompt customization without allowing raw system prompt replacement
bounded_scope: Define additive supervisor prompt overlay profiles, non-overridable invariants, candidate policy and CLI surfaces, and run-log provenance without implementing runtime flags or allowing core prompt replacement.

motivating_concern: external product deployments need stable project-scoped SpecGraph authority rather than self-hosted core evolution
bounded_scope: Define product workspace governance as a locked-core profile for customer projects with project-scoped specs, proposals, diagnostics, implementation work, and artifact publishing without implementing runtime enforcement, hosting topology, or multi-tenant account management.

motivating_concern: product workspace mode is visible but not yet enforced in supervisor move selection or mutation authority
bounded_scope: Define product workspace stable-mode enforcement slices for policy contract tightening, next-move filtering, target/path enforcement, viewer diagnostics, client instance guidance, and smoke coverage without implementing hosted multi-tenancy or automatic upstream export.

motivating_concern: external product workspaces need a safe first-class initialization entrypoint instead of hand-written setup boilerplate
bounded_scope: Define a SpecGraph-owned product workspace initialization contract covering generated layout, project config, setup report, Platform orchestration boundary, SpecSpace status expectations, and SpecPM review-first import boundary without implementing hosted accounts, project switching, or automatic package materialization.

motivating_concern: supervisor operation needs first-class problem diagnosis and deterministic recovery planning instead of ad hoc operator memory
normalized_title: Supervisor Problem Diagnosis and Recovery Planner
bounded_scope: Define a supervisor problem diagnosis artifact, problem vocabulary, safe recovery planner, hard stops, and future operator surface boundary without implementing autopilot, merge authority, or automatic recovery execution.

motivating_concern: supervisor executor behavior is Codex-shaped and needs a pragmatic launch-and-observe boundary before alternate agent CLI experiments
bounded_scope: Define executor adapters as launch-and-observe boundaries with observed capabilities, normalized run results, smoke contracts, and layer separation without replacing Codex, implementing Copilot support, or treating adapters as trust boundaries.

motivating_concern: graph-context chat and viewer-driven request preparation need an implementation-agnostic operator surface boundary rather than hardcoded SpecSpace semantics
bounded_scope: Define Graph Operator Surface as a human-facing request-preparation layer that emits or prepares operator_request_packet handoffs with explicit human confirmation, without implementing UI, autonomous agent loops, or direct canonical graph mutation.

motivating_concern: product feature delivery needs evidence stronger than merged PRs, release notes, or generic analytics events
bounded_scope: Define SpecGraph integration with the external Feature Passport evidence model, including graph links, derived indexes, evidence ladder projection, and honesty boundaries without duplicating the Feature Passport RFC or implementing SDK, ingestion, storage, or UI.

motivating_concern: SpecGraph and SpecSpace already use graph-facing agents without a stable external identity and authority contract
normalized_title: Agent Passport Adoption for Graph Agents
bounded_scope: Define graph-side Agent Passport references, verification gaps, agent surface indexes, and declaration/enforcement boundaries without redefining Agent Passport, signing, sandboxing, hosted registry behavior, or runtime enforcement.

motivating_concern: SpecGraph needs a proposal-first lower semantic import plane for external Ontology packages, compiler outputs, and prompt-agent drafts
bounded_scope: Define graph-side ontology package references, concept references, semantic bindings, import locks, gaps, prompt-pack references, governance evidence and trusted registry gate references, and proposal-only authority boundaries without implementing ontologyc invocation, prompt-agent execution, registry service, Docker packaging, SpecAgent runtime, or SpecSpace UI.

motivating_concern: SpecSpace needs a deterministic proposal materialization debt surface instead of reconstructing proposal debt from lower-level runtime and backlog artifacts
bounded_scope: Define a viewer-facing proposal debt report derived from proposal_runtime_index and graph_backlog_projection, including summary counts, grouped status, missing markers, suggested actions, and safe read-only fields without implementing the report, SpecSpace UI, automatic proposal materialization, or priority changes.

motivating_concern: SpecGraph should turn a small raw product intent into a fast ontology-backed proto-graph without confusing generated placeholders with mature canonical specs
bounded_scope: Define ontology-backed proto-graph seed generation, placeholder node lifecycle, recursive refinement queues, compute and attention budget semantics, and promotion boundaries without implementing the generator, invoking ontologyc, building SpecSpace UI, or auto-mutating canonical specs.
